latestPosts.caption:
Mr. Babs met George when they were 13 years old at Fenwick High School in Oak Park, Illinois, and they’ve remained close friends for the past 64 years.

There is something so special about spending time with friends who have shared a lifetime of memories. Mr. Babs and I recently traveled to Greece with our dear friends George and Marilyn, and it was a trip I’ll never forget.

I met George when I was 17. He’s one of those people with a truly big personality, funny, quick-witted, and the kind of friend who shows up no matter what. Loyal, supportive, and always ready to make you laugh, he’s a rare find!

And Marilyn is the perfect yin to George’s yang. Together, they’ve built a beautiful life and friendship that we’re so grateful to be a part of🇬🇷🤍

Babs hack ✨ If you get stubborn yellowing on your white soles. Try hair developer! Paint volume 40 on the bottoms, wrap the shoes in paper towels and let them soak up some sun for about 3-4 hours, it takes it right out!

Comment BUG and I’ll send you the link to my homemaking book! More than 150 easy home hacks, practical tips, cleaning methods, organizing systems, and seasonal checklists all in one place to help make caring for your home finally feel manageable.

Today’s tip? Keep the bugs away naturally with cinnamon!

INGREDIENTS:
2 teaspoons of cinnamon
4 cups of warm water
A couple of drops of rubbing alcohol a couple of drops of dish soap

INSTRUCTIONS:
Mix cinnamon into the water and set it out for at least a few hours to steep - as if you were making tea. Strain out the liquid to ensure it doesn’t clog up your spray bottle.  Pour cinnamon “tea” into a spray bottle then add the rubbing alcohol and dish soap. Shake to combine and spray.

Fruit Fly Deterrent
For a fruit fly free fruit bowl, just place a couple cinnamon sticks in your fruit bowl and you will be fruit fly free all summer long.

Trash Can Protector
Keep pests, maggots AND raccoons out of your trash cans with cinnamon.  Just sprinkle a light layer at the bottom of the trash can and don’t forget the lid. Those critters hate the smell and the powdery texture of cinnamon.

This Strawberry Shortcake Icebox Cake is a 10/10 🍓✨

INGREDIENTS:
3 lbs strawberries, sliced

4 cups heavy cream or 3 containers Cool Whip

2 tsp vanilla extract (if making homemade whipped cream)

½ cup powdered sugar

1 box honey graham crackers

INSTRUCTIONS:
Wash and slice the strawberries. (If I can’t find organic strawberries, I always use Koala Eco Fruit and Vegetable Wash.)

To make homemade whipped cream, chill the bowl and beaters you plan to use. In the bowl of a stand mixer or using a hand mixer, combine the heavy cream, powdered sugar and vanilla extract. Beat on medium speed until firm peaks form, about 4–6 minutes.

Spread a thin layer of whipped cream on the bottom of your dish. Add one layer of graham crackers. Spread about one-third of the whipped cream over the graham crackers, then top with 1 pound of sliced strawberries.

Repeat the layers two more times: graham crackers, whipped cream, strawberries.

Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight so the graham crackers soften into a cake-like texture. Scoop and serve cold!🇺🇸

Allergy season is one of the worst we’ve seen in years. If allergies have you feeling less than your best, here are 5 simple things you can do to ease the misery:

🌿 Wear a hat and sunglasses when you go outside. A hat helps keep pollen from settling in your hair, and sunglasses protect your eyes from allergens in the air. If you forgot the hat, try using a cool blow dryer to help remove some of the pollen.

🚿 Shower at night instead of in the morning. This helps wash away pollen that’s built up on your skin and hair throughout the day, so you’re not sleeping in it all night long.

👃 Swipe a little olive oil inside your nose. It can help create a barrier to trap allergens before they irritate your nasal passages.

💧 Use a nasal rinse at least once a day. Just be sure to use distilled water only.

👁️ Put allergy eye drops in before bed. This gives them time to work overnight, so you wake up with less irritation and itchiness.

Allergy season can be brutal, but hopefully these little tricks help you breathe a little easier. 🌼

❤️ WEDDING ETIQUETTE ❤️ Did you know?!

It’s Wedding Season! Mr. Babs and I LOVE weddings, and over the years we’ve learned a few etiquette rules that make the day even more special for the happy couple 🤍

❤️ RSVP on time. Typically you know if you are going or not, so RSVP right away. Weddings take an enormous amount of planning and when you respond in a timely fashion, it helps the couple tremendously. Also, if you are not specifically invited with a guest or plus one, please don’t bring one. The same goes for children unless they are specifically included on the invitation.

❤️ Don’t be late. Try to arrive about 15 minutes before the ceremony is set to begin.

❤️ Stick to the registry if there is one. Never choose a wedding gift based on your own taste. The registry is there for a reason! A thoughtful gift the couple actually asked for is always appreciated. And if the gift is larger than a card, send it to their home rather than bringing it to the reception.

❤️ Don’t be a wedding dud. Be social, celebrate the couple, and for goodness sake… DANCE! Get out on that dance floor and enjoy yourself. Weddings are meant to be joyful.

Most importantly, remember that a wedding is about celebrating love and supporting the couple as they begin their life together 🤍

XO,
Babs and Mr. Babs

I became a mom nearly 51 years ago, and I truly believe motherhood has been  my greatest calling  in life. Like all young families at the time, we figured out Mother’s Day as we went along. It actually took me some time to discover what an “ideal” Mother’s Day looked like for me.  And truthfully, I’m not even sure I know to this day.

I was always the kind of mom who simply wanted to be with her children on Mother’s Day… despite the expectations versus the reality. Through the years, Mr. Babs always did his best to think of the little things he knew would bring me joy. Oh, how I miss those messy, beautiful, wonderful years.

Still, each year, I feel so honored to celebrate Mother’s Day with my children and now my children’s children.
Happy Mother’s Day to each of you. May you take a moment to dig deep and discover what will make you feel most honored and loved. And above all, may you truly feel the love your family holds for you this Mother’s Day.❤️

Comment “Spring” and I’ll send you all the recipes from our Spring Party 🌸

This past weekend, we celebrated two of my grandsons’ First Holy Communions. Talk about a special occasion!

We have all been looking forward to this most important day and wanted to celebrate it in a truly worthy manner. The boys had a few special requests, which made it even more fun.

What made it especially enjoyable was the fact that these recipes can easily be made ahead of time. The perfect menu to relieve the stress on party day. 

We celebrated First Communions but this menu is your go-to for any spring event or get together since there’s nothing quite like a spring outdoor party. 

Our Spring Menu:

Whole Grilled Filet of Beef with my Red Chimichurri

Grilled Salmon Salad

Roasted Smashed Potatoes with Green Goddess Dipping Sauce

Eileen’s Strawberry Salad

Orzo Asparagus Salad

Strawberry Shortcake Sheet Cake

First Communion Cupcake Cross

#IZERVAYPartner Let’s talk about something that doesn’t get checked often enough: your eyesight. Geographic atrophy (GA) is an advanced form of dry age-related macular degeneration (AMD), and sometimes, it shows no symptoms at all. 
My family has a history of dry AMD, so I make it a point to stay informed and proactive. As an @izervay partner, I’ve learned about how this condition can progress, and I think everyone should do the same. Visiting an eye doctor regularly is the best way to detect GA early and discuss a treatment option like IZERVAY.
Visit @izervay_pi for Prescribing Information. See scrolling Important Safety Information in video.
Don’t take IZERVAY if you have an infection or active swelling in or around your eye. IZERVAY can cause eye infection, retinal detachment, or increased risk of wet AMD. IZERVAY may cause blood in the white of the eye, blurred vision, or a temporary increase in eye pressure. Do not drive or use machinery until vision has recovered after an eye injection or exam.
#GeographicAtrophy #IZERVAY ��For U.S. audiences only.

My new book, Homemaking with Babs, is officially available for PREORDER! Link in bio 🤍
After all these years of running a home and sharing my favorite tips and tricks online, I’ve finally gathered everything I’ve learned into one place. From my daily routines to DIY cleaning recipes and the home systems that truly keep things running smoothly.  This book is filled with what actually works.
Whether you’re in a apartment or a house, no matter the size of the space, this book is for you. I’ll walk you through simple daily habits, seasonal checklists, and easy ways to keep your home running without feeling overwhelmed.
✨ My “Clean in 15” routines (because it shouldn’t take all day!)
✨ Seasonal cleaning and organizing guides
✨ Practical home maintenance tips
✨ Natural DIY cleaners and little tricks that really work
✨ Ways to get the whole family involved (yes, even the kids!)
Preorder your copy.  I can’t wait for this book to be in your home.  XO Babs
